name: NRMA Treasure Island Holiday Resort, Gold Coast
address: 117 Brisbane Rd, Biggera Waters QLD 4216, Australia
all_reviews_text:
It was a great family holidays at NRMA and the staff was super friendly. Would highly recommend NRMA Treasure Island.

We stayed at treasure island last week for the first time and had a great experience overall. The first night was a bit rough with some issues with our selected cabin, but the staff were great about it and quick to rectify it in the morning upgrading us to another cabin. The lagoon poolside villa was perfect for our family, spacious, clean and comfortable beds. My kids loved this park so much with all the activities and especially the main pool with waterslides. Staff were really accommodating and helpful, particularly with my youngest who has special needs. We met some lovely families during our stay and my daughters made alot of friends/memories. We will definitely be looking forward to staying again.

Just spent the long weekend here.
The facility's are great even in the rain we had a good time with our toddler.

Only a couple downs falls.
- they only have 1 family bathroom which was quite a mission to get to and wasn't the best set up.

And the biggest down fall people SMOKING everywhere. I really don't like my toddler around cigarette smoke. It was at the site right next to up, near the pools and the splash pad.
Was really disappointing & wpuld stop me coming back.

There are so many family friendly activities and it is a spacious caravan park with 4 pools and even a pool attendant on site. Great value and fun for families. Would stay again!

Great location, easy and convenient online check in, clean and well maintained.
Stayed on a powered site in our tent.  Was a great set up.

No internet on tv
No wet weather program for children
Park amenities were amazing just a shame we couldn’t use them due to weather
Staff were excellent
Studio rooms could use a freshen up

We had a great stay here last month. Park was clean, lots of activities for the children on our chill days. The kids loved the pool and wreck room. Staff were lovely, room was clean but a little run down. Only issue I really had was our cabin was under a tree which bats live in, and our car was covered in tree stuff and bat poop constantly, was annoying. Also constantly woken up by the tree smacking against the roof of the cabin. Aside from that we had a great stay!

This is the best caravan park I’ve been to in Australia for families. They have lots of activities where you can win free go karts, ice blocks or even kids meals for the on-site restaurant.
Me and my family have been here a few times and it’s been terrific every time. Five stars for sure!

For what it is the park was ok.  We didnt use the restaurant or Cafe, we didnt use the amenities as we had an powered ensuite site.  After discussion on more than 1 or 2 occasions with staff and the length of our rig and our large tow vehicle we were disappointed to get the small length site.  With the ensuite between 2 sites 1 has extra room at the back by several meters.
The access roads and space to actually get into the sites is very tight.  We were lucky no one was in the sites opposite us and that gave us extra room.  No idea how we would get in if the park was full.
Disappointed that as our site was on the opposite side of the park and I have mobility disabilities we couldn't drive to the pool, hense we couldn't access the pool.
Every staff member we spoke to were fantastic.  We travel with cats and 2 cleaners came up to ask if they could take a photo with the cats.  Was so nice to actually be asked first before they took photos.  We gave them cuddles with 1 cat who is super friendly quickly.  The cat lives meeting new friends.
Location wise its really hard to turn right when coming out of the park.  Particularly with a big van in tow.  The reason for our trip required us to take the van in for an assessment which was local and why we chose the park.  We did prefer the big 4 gold coast as the location is easier to get in and out of the park.

Our stay has been wet and at the end of the April you would think they would have the heated pools on considering the amount of kids here and the weather! There are renovations going on so everything looks messy, NO activities or drawing tables set up in the wreck room, the play ground and jumping pillow is shut due to the rain so unless you want to spend a lot on the 3 arcade games this place is extremely not kid friendly at the end of April. And spite of all of this no one has common sense to turn on the pool heaters as there is no manager on site. If I could rate 0 star I would. Worst holiday ever! PS just turn on the heated pools and set activities up alot of people have paid good money to be here and have fun not sit in their dated accommodation.

Great place staff are very helpful and friendly and big bonus next to harbour town

Amazing caravan park the pool was nice with the little water slide 🛝 mini golf was great too

There's a Pub at this caravan park right next to a splash park, need I say more?

We stayed here with family last year and have re booked again for this year . We had an absolutely fantastic time here with everything you need right here or a short distance away .
The accommodation was great both cabins We had were very clean and updated . The park has that many amenities you dont have to leave of you dont want to your children can have so much fun all day long .

Great family friendly park , we didn’t have to leave for keep the kids entertained

The decibels of the music and children's entertainment was so high that we had to move sites because our children couldn't cope with the noise. Staff accommodated the move which was great.
We moved sites only to go where we were opposite what seem to be "residents" who stayed up way beyond 10:00 pm drunkenly and loudly talking.
When you make a booking, you are made to sign a lengthy list of conditions that say coarse language is not to be used. I have heard more F words in two days here than the rest of our trip to Queensland. Not the staff, but loud parents and teenagers.
The pools are not at all large enough for the amount of users. The pirate theme is almost nonexistent.
The tall man on staff is very good at his job, hence the one star.

We had a fantastic experience here.
Customer service over the phone was helpful, taking the stress out of our last min campsite booking. Toilets are pin locked for that extra level of safety and security.
Great amenities and facilities with heaps to do (especially if you have kids). The bistro food was delicious, filling and well priced. Would happily stay here again in a heartbeat.

Had such an amazing week here. Such a big park and so much to do, great clean facilities, friendly staff and lovely accommodation.
I’d say the only downside was being so close to the road but it felt very safe and enclosed. And otherwise location is great for getting to the shops for anything you need.

Great park, plenty of things for families to do and close to all the theme parks. Would definitely stay again

Stayed for 7 days

Great place to come with kids although they was a little bit disappointed they did have two spas there obviously they broke instead of replacing them took the cheap option and put a crappy food van in the spot with a bunch of tables exposed to the elements. my wife uses the disabled toilet clearly the lock had broken at some stage and instead of replacing it they just removed it so that anyone else who had the code to that toilet could walk in on somebody in the shower or sitting on the toilet obviously don’t like spending money

Great place to stay with kids of all ages. Craft activities and park for the little ones, basketball, tennis and go-karts for the bigger ones. Convenient location right near Harbourtown outlets. Staff were all so lovely and helpful.

This is our 3rd Easter at this park and it will be our last.  While it was nice to have the R & R our kids are too old now (15 and 13) for the majority of the organized activities here.  There's a huge difference in price between cabins and caravan sites, I'm talking hundreds of dollars.  Food and drink is ridiculously overpriced ($6 for one tiny scoop of Peter's ice-cream; minus the cone, $5 for a can of soft drink AND with a 15% surcharge on top.  You could hardly call the place a licensed venue, as there's very little choice in the way of alcoholic beverages, unless you include the cocktails at $24 a pop and the one staff member making them and serving customers too.  The staff do what they can, they are friendly and approachable, the pools and amenities were clean, refreshing and tidy, but it was just a little flat for us and hard to find the value for our money this year.  I don't wish to begrudge little kids being little kids by hogging the pool and hot tubs and eating their own snot, so we're looking forward to trying somewhere else for Easter next year.

Great resort with great atmosphere stayed in a lagoon villa great for the kids couldn't compliment the staff and resort more highly.

Perfect for families.

This place is awesome to stay at from the staff to the activities,  to the relax atmosphere, sometimes you forget that there is more to see in the Gold Coast cause once you pass the gates life is relaxing and everyone are just so happy...
The room we had was the deluxe villa right next to the pool and honestly we hardly heard anything. The room was absolutely perfect
If im in the GC again I will ve staying here again

Staff were incredible Food in the restaurant was delicious
We loved all of the facilities for the kids and family run programs our room was very clean with a nice view of the pool
Will definitely stay again

Look location is good. Walk to the DFO and other shops.  Sent a text to say room was ready which was great. Stayed in a pool side villa.  For nearly $400 a night I expected more.  The bunk bed mattresses were very uncomfortable and my kids slept on the couch instead.  We have no wifi reception in the villa. Which was a bit annoying.  The fan in the main bed was so noisy you actually couldn't sleep with it on.  Pools were good but busy and plenty of activities for kids to do.  The mini golf was free so that was nice.  We had breakfast at the restaurant. nothing special, chef did half the order wrong so had to wait for it to be re cooked. The villa could do with a bit of love, as it had seen better days.

Enjoyed our stay. We had an issue with our room but the staff quickly attended to this which we appreciated.

I am disabled,  I was told its a family park and I'm not welcome. I booked in, walk across the street to get water proof spray. I return to my car to find a staff member asking me to leave, because I left the car unattended for 15min. Distuguting staff. The park is nice but the way guests are treated very poorly. Most attractions are worn out or broken. I have stayed here before, but never again

Site was too small for our van and couldn't get the swing to reverse into site

We stayed on a Premium Powered Site - Caravan/Tentand and got allocated site 254 for one night. As soon as it got dark the bats in the palm trees stated stripping the berries and making an awful mess and sound. Woke up in the morning to our motor home absolutely covered in mess, which took us over an hour to clean before check out.

The site next to us was the same. They were there for 3 nights and requested for a new sites.

3 stars because we would absolutely stay again but would avoid sites 254, 253.

Location great - walking distance to many things, including Coles.

Facilities great - pools, spa, splash pad, bikes, jumping pillow, playground. Clean amenities and laundry facilities were ok, although dryer still took 3 cycles (on HIGH as directed on a note), but that's ok.

Purchased a large number of milkshakes and smoothies and they were terrible, tasted like water.  Even the kids wouldn't drink them.  No flavour at all.  Won't be back there

Fantastic park, great staff, dog friendly and very clean amenitys, will be returning in Febuarry 2026. 🤪👍🐶🇦🇺🇮🇱❤️
Back in April for my 3rd visit 👍👍🤪🐶🇦🇺

Alle Mitarbeiter am Empfang sehr nett. Für Kinder gibt es viele Beschäftigungsmöglichkeiten. Eher ein lauter Park, da er sehr gross ist und viele Familien diesen Park buchen. Wir hatten Glück und bekamen einen Platz in einer ruhigen Ecke. Die Minigolf - Anlage hätte eine Auffrischung nötig und weitere Abwaschmöglichkeiten wäre auch kein Luxus.

My husband and I stayed here for one night!
The procedure for booking online was very quick and then I let them know of our late check-in. when we arrived I called a guy and in 2 mins was at the reception. The pitch was in a very good position, near the facilities and the toilets have a code to enter so very safe.
The only “bad” thins was that the WiFi didn’t work :(
But we recommend!!

We visited this park 20 years ago and loved it, retuning now we were blown away by the improvements that have been made. Special shout out to the kiwi female on reception upon check in. I’m so sad I didn’t get her name and the female with the nose piercing at the cafe was so kind and lovely. Great experience for us all

So much fun for kids!! Definitely worth it!

Awesome park has everything u need quite busy but that's ok really nice people

We recently stayed at a Ensuite Site, this is our third time at the park and their facilities and proximity to attractions is fantastic.

Had a great week with all the family
Grandkids loved the pools and play area and the pizzas
Can’t wait to come back

We have stayed here two years in a row for a family holiday and it's such a nice place for the kids and adults. Alot of things for kids to do they loved the bouncy pillow and pools. We enjoyed being able to have a bite to eat at the onsite restaurant. All the staff are lovely and we will always go back

Treasure Island has been one of our best stays by far, the many pools and variation for everyone keeps it interesting and so fun for the kids. Good amenities, camp kitchen and bathrooms, great activities for the kids on the weekends etc movies so much fun. Super friendly staff and just overall a great location so easy to get to everywhere on the Gold Coast. We will definitely be back!

Definitely recommend. Perfect place if you have young ones, lots of activities for them to do. The bar and swimming pools are awesome. Very close to Harbour town.
Don't need to leave, because everything is pretty much there to enjoy your holiday.

This was a great place to stay with a family. We stayed in a cabin that slept 9 comfortably. It was well supplied with everything we needed.
The resort activities for kids are amazing and the staff all so friendly

A great holiday location for the family, but I ended up with severe bed bug bites after staying for just one night. My arms and legs are still covered in bites almost 1 week later, which is devastating because we thoroughly enjoyed our stay otherwise.

Brilliant accommodation food and location kids had a ball cocktail hour was awesome  bar staff and chefs  amazing 👏

We try and stay here every yr for the kids birthdays. Always something fun for the kids and adults to do.. even if its just relaxing in or by the pool..

We had the best holiday. This park was amazing the kids had an absolute ball, there was so much for them to do here they made friends everyone at the park was so friendly. The park was so clean its definately a hidden paradise. Were already thinking about booking our next holiday the kids didn’t want to leave. Thanks for our amazing stay and what made it even better we had a cabin with washer and dryer so now weve arrived home theres no washing to do just put the clothes away. This has been the best holiday experience as a family of 5 with a baby. Thanks guys see you next time

We recently hired a room at Treasure Island and unfortunately had a very disappointing experience.

Shortly after arriving, my son began severely sneezing, rubbing his nose, and his eyes were watering. When I looked around the room, I noticed that all three ceiling fans in the were caked in thick dust. After climbing up to check, it was clear the dust had built up over what appeared to be years.

I then checked the air conditioning unit. The filters looked as though they hadn’t been cleaned in a very long time, and there was visible black mould throughout the entire unit. I know this to be mould as i work with HVAC systems And these units at treasure Island don’t look to have had a proper deep pressure clean for at least 5 years (at a minimum), We stayed during hot weather and were unable to use either the fans or the air conditioning due to the level of dust and toxic mould buildup.

For the price we paid per night, this was incredibly disappointing. While the floors, benches, and bathrooms appeared surface-cleaned, it was evident that no consistent deep cleaning or maintenance is being done on essential items like fans and air conditioning units.

I would also recommend guests check the kettles provided. Ours was a white plastic kettle, and the interior plastic was completely black with buildup. I had to purchase citric acid from the shop to properly clean it before we felt comfortable using it.

From the outside, the resort presents as a great place to stay. However, once inside, it felt like only very basic surface cleaning had been completed. I strongly suggest management review their maintenance and deep-cleaning processes, particularly for air conditioning systems, ceiling fans, and kitchen appliances.

For future guests, I recommend checking air conditioning units for mould, inspecting ceiling fans and surrounding areas for dust buildup, and checking kettles before use.

Very disappointing experience overall.

Cheap and cheerful. Rooms could do with an update but for a budget friendly place handy to buses and harbour town this would be hard to beat.

We absolutely loved our 9 day stay at Treasure Island. The resort has great facilities, lots of activities for young and old and the accommodation suitable for every one. We look forward to returning.

Wife/I stayed here for a couple of nights with our young children. Our first trip away as a family and we primarily wanted somewhere the kids would have fun.||Stayed in a two bedroom villa. It was very clean. Fairly dated though. Everything we needed for our stay was there, although must say the beds were super uncomfortable and made for poor sleep.||At all touchpoints, the staff were courteous and decent to speak with.||For what's served, the food from the bistro is fairly exey. A number of alternate takeaway options are a short walk away. Having a drink on the bistro verandah in the arvo was pleasant.||The kids enjoyed the bounce pillow and the pool area. Pool area gets crowded quickly, and needs more seating. I appreciate the park having kids activities available each day, but they were pretty mid.||As the title says, this park is ok, but not for us. We'll choose somewhere else for our next short trip.

Best stay we had 100% would recommend and will be back

Great place for family activities at very convenient location for Gold Coast fun

We stayed in the safari tent and what a treat! The staff were so lovely and kind and the kids had a blast! Also loved our visitor that came up super close! Thank you team for having us!

I can't speak for a winter experience but in Summer if you're looking for somewhere where the children will have plenty to do, look no further!
Pools (one with a water slide). Karts. Mini-golf. Tennis/Basketball court. A mini arcade.
And all the fun activities at the Wreck Room, many of which are run by the Treasure Island Crew.
The only real downfall was the Wi-Fi was horrible. But that should be the least of anybodies worries when on holidays.

GREAT FAMILY VIBE!
This holiday park had it all! Activities were great and had a range for all ages, The Amenities were always clean and well maintained. We didn't have to leave the park which is exactly what we were wanting.
One of our favourite family holidays.

Loved everything and everyone

Great park, clean, neat and tidy. Plenty for the kids to do. Staff are lovely. Restaurant is great and good value.
Have stayed multiple time and will be staying again.

2nd time staying and absolutely loved it, the staff is incredibly friendly accidentally left medication in the room upon checkout and they called me to let me know!, facilities were great my daughter loved the waterslide and mocktails! The room was super clean as a cleaner myself I know how hard it is through the summer days so well done to the cleaners and to the entire staff in general, definitely recommend for a family holiday

Best caravan park in Australia friendly staff and awesome facilities been here many times and will def be returning

Great place,great people, great service. What more could you want.

Our family had a lovely stay. Our three year old really enjoyed the swimming pools, playground, jumping pillow and waterpark. The bed was also very comfortable. We will be back.

Caitlin and Mia are so so so funny and excellent at their jobs. They deserve so much money and adoration !!!! GIVE THEM A RAISE

Caitlin & Mia are sooooooo awesome and cool and funny!

We stayed in a 2 bedroom, 2 bathroom ensuite, pandanus villa. These rooms are very spacious for a family of 4, with your own deck with a bbq, outdoor dining, spacious living, kitchen and dining inside, and own laundry facilities. Bonus having 2 bathrooms. This is our second visit and I must say I was a little dissapointed with some things.
The park was extremely over crowded which was expected being school holidays, but that also meant pools were packed on hot days like a sardine tin. No shade on pools which is a little annoying. But the biggest downfall for the park is the outdoor furniture, since staying last time the lay back pool seats have been swapped out for plastic cane furniture with white cushioning, for the pool and public areas. As you can imagine the seats have seen better days, they are stained, ripped and every seat cracked or a huge hole and not able to be used. This made it harder to sit beside the pool and watch the kids. We  went to play mini golf after Dinner but lighting wasn't great on all over the course which made it hard to see some of the holes. But the course is in desperate need of a clean up, the greens are covered in leaves and debris. One of our mornings we went to buy a hot chocolate and coffee, we were a little shocked at the price when paying at almost $17 for 2 drinks. Then having to return a few mins later as the hot chocolate tasted like warm milky water. On a plus they have the karts to hire for kids. The basket ball, tennis court, table tennis, mini golf, the night time cinema on the grass, play ground, jumping pillow and splash park are all free to use. Pools are open early until 9pm. As most parks do, they have rules which are great, but no one polices them for example, no kids wear helmets while on scooters or bikes, every second kid was on electric scooters or ebikes in the park. I was watching my kids at the splash park on two occasions witnessing adults and children taking their dogs into the splash park. With staff looking on and saying nothing. Another plus is harbour town only a few mins walk from the front gate, hungry jacks across the road, woolworths up the road, coles down the road. Surfers paradise only 10kms away. And theme parks only 10 kms away in the other direction.
I would still visit again but pools need some shade and definitely need new furniture, possibly hard wearing not plastic and no white cushions.

Disappointed with the state of our site and the amount of rubbish we had to pick up to make it safe for our one year old, we stayed shortly after new years and mentioned it to the workers in the office, and we pretty much got a blunt reply, not much of an apology or any other look over. We saw multiple people leave the following day and arrive the same day and noticed no one came to check over those sites either. Also very disappointed with the fact that you can smoke wherever! It was raining and a bit cold so we headed to the spas and couldn’t even stay because a male staying in the cabin a few feet away from the only spa pools was smoking and refused to move after asking because it is allowed. Facilities are great but definitely won’t be staying again as I don’t agree and want my one year old breathing in cigarette smoke throughout the whole park. Pool staff were lovely.

Great campsite this one. It has fantastic amenities for the children, such as 3x pools, splash park, mini putt-putt, jumping pillow!, a nice restaurant with a bar (a little overpriced though) and plenty of space to go for a walk etc. The beach is a stones throw away with Harbourtown just across the road. There is only two spas though, there used to be a few more I believe.
We will definitely be back!

This is the second time I have stayed here. A great place to stay for the kids! They had a ball, so many activities! All Staff were amazing, from bar, kitchen staff and activity staff. They do need a bigger budget to buy better pens for the bingo though! We all enjoyed the bingo nights and mini golf.

The only downside is that the hot spas near the main pool have been removed. That was my favourite part about the first time I stayed! I was so disappointed that they have been replaced by an icecream truck. They have still have a couple near another pool, but they were luke warm and not as many jets as the old ones (If I recall correctly). I'm not sure if the temperature was turned down due to it being summer? Last time I stayed it was Autumn. We loved the pools anyway. Overall a fantastic few days, thank you!

Great place for kids. Lots of activities at the resort for kids and adults. Bistro food was good too - like pub food.
Ebike, and pedal cars available for hire but is expensive.
Wifi didn't work in the room unfortunately. Room was good but maybe a little tired. Everything worked though. Laundry available but stained my clothes and only took $1 coins (cost $4/load).
Stayed in the lagoon villa. Three bedrooms of good size plus a bed in the upstairs living room. Air conditioning in both living rooms and fans in the bedrooms. Spa and pool right behind room which was lovely.
Would recommend overall.

Thank you for making our holiday one to remember! We loved staying here on the Gold Coast! ☀️ My kids loved playing mini golf, jumping on the jumping pillow, arcade games, tennis, splash park, ping-pong and go karts! We had such a wonderful stay! If you have kids it’s a great place with so many options! We found this place so close to theme parks and shops! We were central to everything! ☀️ thank you again for having us!

I’ll be completely honest I had seen somewhere it was a luxury resort and I was expecting such, it is basically a huge  campground with townhouses to stay in, it’s not luxury but it’s nice enough.
the pools were great my only observation  is shade,  everyone crammed into the smaller pool under the only shade on the really hot days. all the kids activities were awesome, mine were amused at all times and the bigger ones had the beach just down the road or harbourtown next door!!
We stayed in a 3 bedroom villa and we had a number of issues which definitely put a dampener on the stay, however the staff were incredible super accomodating, came after hours, did everything in their power to resolve issues, communication was amazing they refunded a night without being asked because of the issues. There was an exceptionally rude lady that worked in the bar but other than her no complaints.
Would I stay again, I would have said probably not but honestly the way they handled our complaint and the level of service they gave us I think I probably would, the villa was roomy and nice, the beds were comfy, sitting on our balcony was lovely and the friendly magpie that made itself at home kept everyone amused!!  Thank you for our stay and your service

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
We had an amazing stay at NRMA Treasure Island and wish we could have stayed longer!
We stayed in one of the safari tents, which was comfortable, well set up, and a real highlight of our trip.
There were plenty of fun activities running throughout the day, and the activities team were absolutely fantastic — so friendly and full of energy, making it fun for all ages.
The location is great, making it easy to relax and enjoy everything the park has to offer.
Having the bistro on site was a big bonus, with really good food and a great atmosphere. Overall, it was a wonderful experience and we can’t wait to come back one day.
Highly recommend!

Great place to stay. Lots to do. Coming back. Family holidays at this place from now on.

Friendly staff, food is delicious I got the bbq ribs perfectly cooked. So many fun things for the kids to do and hands down the bed is super comfy most hotels beds are hard as a rock. It’s such a awesome place looking forward to staying next time

We had no contact with staff everything was sent through email very sad

We stayed in a Villa. 2 bedroom, 2 bathrooms.
Accommodation was nice. Beds comfortable.
Kitchen was a decent size. There was a window in the kitchen that people could see straight through - needs a blind or needs to be tinted.
It was good to have a washer and dryer. However, the washer machine had mould in it.
There is a boat ramp but not a very good one. Not recommended for boat, we ended up driving down to Southport.
Staff were friendly. Let us use a camp-site to clean our boat.
Location not the best. Great that it is near Harbour Town. However too far from Surfers Paradise and the Boat ramp let it down.

The resort has a good location, being nice and close to shops, which was very convenient. The pool area itself is spacious, and the showers and spa area were clean and enjoyable.

However, the restaurant was quite expensive for what was offered. The pool area also had hardly anywhere to sit, which made it difficult during busy times. A major downside was that the dryers did not work properly — the resort should really invest the money they earn into upgrading and maintaining essential facilities like this.

Staff were mostly fine and friendly, though one lady was quite rude, which was disappointing. We were only trying to help empty the golf ball box for the kids and she came over screaming almost abusive. Had to tell her to stop and leave. (Short brown hair, large build) Overall, there are some nice aspects to the resort, but a few improvements would make a big difference to the overall experience.

The location is good if you want to go shopping: the staff in the bistro/bar may need some refreshers in customer service. Ordered a coffee, lady hands me a number. I said, it’s ok I’ll wait: she tells me take the number I’ll come and find u: sure thanks I said. We wait and wait. Never saw her looking outside for us. Went back to the counter and she says “what do you want me to do look everywhere for u” i said I was just out there never did we see you come outside. No one where’s name badges. Had another experience where the staff forgot to lock up the ride on bikes, we tried to move them back off the road: he comes out saying, you are suppose to come and tell us!! Sorry what we were trying to get them to not run over the kids. Same girl tells us to not bring alcohol on the decking! Place the cap back on. No signs anywhere saying that. Instead of to say finish it off. If we had of stayed longer we would’ve purchased 4 drinks from the bar: instead we left the trivia.
Just lacking customer service:

Whilst we spent $1500 on accommodation and are NRMA members. On checkout day; it was 10.10am the worker comes around “are we almost done”! 10minutes past 10am!!!

The activities team had a good vibe on them and were entertaining.

An amazing place central to the majority of Theme Parks down the Coast. Lots of activities for children and adults alike at the resort itself.

Kids had the best time ! Staff were extremely friendly . We stayed in the superior garden villa and was perfect for the 4 of us . Deffintely be returning . The grounds are extremely clean and lots of activities for the kids !

Positiiviset:
- Lapsille paljon aktiviteetejä esim. minigolf, uimaaltaat…

Negatiiviset:
- Rauhaton alue
- Yleistilat epäsiistit
- Ympäristö oli teollisuusaluemainen
- Kaukana rannasta

Absolutely fantastic.  Well worth the visit.  So many activities and things to do. Water and land fun for everyone. Not over the top restricted. Food was reasonable and well priced. We were housed where we asked to be and upgraded. Well done on running this place. We will gladly be back

The disability access studio was unsafe. The wide door jamb so close to the round  door handle injured my wifes hand.  The round handle lock needs to be replaced with a lever handle. The room lacked privacy because one of the four panels of the door blinds was missing. The most dangerous thing was that the bathroom had very slippery floor tiles.  The bathroom fan was not working. Damage to the hair dryer lead had not been properly repaired. These things are a breach of Work Health and safety laws.

We had a caravan site, but have also been in the cabins. Great family park with lots of activities for the children. Three pools, tennis, mini golf peddle carts jumping pillow and many extras. A bar and restaurant if you don't feel like cooking and they will do take away. Close to Harbour Town shopping complex and not far to catch the light rail into Surfers. Plenty of major tourist attractions close by. Great spot for a holiday.

Highway Robbery at these prices. $100 pr night for a powered caran site $107for Maui alone is just absolutely ridiculous !!!  You people need to be audited and see who's benefitting the most from the costs on a site of grass !!! What an absolute JOKE ... I would never recommend this park !!!  Gold diggers... No budget for families here, just a complete RIP OFF !!!!like all of your parks !!!

I go camping often, and this was the best place I’ve ever been.

We recently stayed at NRMA Treasure Island in Biggera Waters on a powered caravan site near the amenities block and absolutely loved it. The park has so much to offer. Three beautiful pools, two hot tubs, and a fun water park for the kids. There is also putt putt and plenty of entertainment to keep the little ones busy. The amenities were clean, the shower heads were fantastic, a real step above most parks, and the camp kitchen looked amazing. The onsite bistro and bar were great and the staff were friendly and very accommodating. I absolutely loved this place and cannot wait to return.

We stayed as a family of 3.
Having a restaurant onsite that's open every day is the great.  It is close to lots of places to find great food out as well though.
The other thing they do great is the mostly free kids activities that run throughout the day, every day!

I wanted to love this place but didn't come close to it. It's not horrible but everything is just old and run down. There was rubbish through the splash pad where the kids played. No shade over any of the kids areas - splash pad, bouncing pillow, play ground, mini golf. All in the scorching hot sun.

The pools were ok but small and busy. The spas also had rubbish and undies in them.

The staff were hit and miss. A few lovely (shout out to the pool attendant on Thursday afternoon, the young man behind the bar, the blonde lady in the kitchen, and the lady in the golf car checking the kids on go karts were ok) and a few were grumpy and abrupt.

The rooms were clean as they could be but again just a bit old and run down feeling. The 1 small aircon wasn't able to do much cooling in the Queensland summer heat. We upgraded to a superior room.

I know it's a caravan type resort but we go to lots of these. It would have been fantastic when it first opened, the theming is there. It is just screaming out for some love and upgrades to make it great again and to compete in the market today.

Great value for the GC. $68 a night for powered site, lovely grass well maintained and friendly service

Awesome!!!

The tv was terrible my kids tried to watch YouTube but the internet did not work

Stayed here in a cabin for 2 nights in November. The staff were friendly and very helpful. It's handy having a cafe/restaurant on site. The meals were great. There is also a supermarket a short walk away. The cabins were clean and tidy with crisp fresh sheets and soft towels. I had everything I needed.

Pools were nice but the place was pretty run down and the toilets and showers were constantly dirty during our 3 night stay.

Stayed for 3PTag weekend. An absolute pleasure. We had 4 teenage boys playing and they enjoyed the pools and the spas. It was a bit too rainy to have a go on a couple of other activities. But next time. Bistro was serving yummo food. And had a couple of nice cocktails. But all in all recommended staying here. Over any of the other places to stay at in runaway bay. This is the place to stay!

Great family stay Everything you want under one roof Birds abit noisy Friendly staff

Great holiday park, very spacious and comfortable bed.

We drove up from Victoria for our first family getaway with our 11 month old baby.

We will DEFINITELY be coming back here when the little one is older.
We would HIGHLY reccomend any families to stay here, even a couple or young adults would thrive! Absolutely amazing experience!

Always friendly Staff. The rooms are quite comfortable and suit our needs.

Always a pleasure to visit at this place, very clean and welcoming

Great park, the Lady in the cafe is rude & un professional, cabin was great. The let dowm was the rude. Wouldnt recommend going to the onsite cafe.

Fabulous place to stay overnight and stay the week lots of swimming pools and enjoyment areas

One of the best places to stay especially with kids. Lots to do there and close to the theme parks.

No califico habitación porqué estuve en motorhome, los servicios muy buenos y la ubicación un poco alejada del centro pero no es mala

Our go to for family holidays, has great programs & crafts to keep the kids busy. The restaurant & bar are well situated, very relaxing atmosphere.

We recently stayed for a 3-day family trip. The facilities were clean and well-maintained — bathrooms, camp kitchen, and amenities were all spotless. We appreciated the daily activities for kids, movie nights, free coffee, and the three swimming pools which helped avoid crowding even during the school holidays.

However, there were some disappointing experiences. After playing at the splash pad, both my son and another child developed gastro symptoms, requiring medical care — we suspect the water quality may need checking. The sandfly (midge) issue was severe; our family had over 200 bites combined and needed medication. The bouncy pillow and Central Park areas also had sharp gravel that hurt our feet.

While we enjoyed some aspects of the stay, we hope the management can improve insect control, water hygiene, and surface safety to make it more family-friendly.

Excellent place to stay for all ages especially families, they have so many amenities to keep the children busy while you sit back and relax. The park was extremely clean and far exceeded our expectations.It’s budget friendly and the main bed was like sleeping on a cloud.

Good fun, bathroom door jammed needs fixing,, lots of rubbish under lounge suite.  But children loved it.

hmm beds are terrible they suck you in and twist your back no support. parking has no structure and you never get a park near your room. food is overpriced and average. the guys at front desk  were great and the first security guard but when we checked out I parked mycar in the car park area and a guy honked his horn instantly and then came and took photos of my licence plate and all around my car and treated us like we just stole something  and we stayed there for 8 days. he needs to be fired big time so rude threatening disposition and all I was doing was returning the key. my 3kids were in the car scared of him . in short rooms dated pools are good for kids most staff good friendly but one. not happy

We did see roaches in the unit so pest control would be appreciated. The kids club staff were friendly. Location is good with only a 5 minute drive to the coast and walking distance of shops, restaurants and takeaways.

The only thing going for this place was the location to visit family showing it age the site was so small could not put the van annex out because of the van on the next sit had a slide out on it taking up.part of my sit children play ground rubber soft fall had peace missing and lifting

Easy check in, lots of great activities for the kids, mine especially loved the mini golf! The beds were comfortable and we really enjoyed the restaurant too, I had a curry dish and it was really delightful! Breakfast was really reasonably priced too, a great all round experience here!

Great holiday park with plenty of amenities, activities and events. Great for both the big kids and the littlies

The family studio cabin was incredibly welcoming upon arrival. It's beautifully styled and seemed almost brand new. The bedding configuration was a bit strange however. Okay

Great facilities..

While this holiday park is in a strange location, it’s excellent for families. |Despite being on the main road, there’s no noise in the cabins. |The park has excellent facilities and there’s plenty for families to do to keep entertained.

It’s a good park for families as there are activities to keep children entertained. We stayed on a powered site which was a good size for our large rig. Great holiday destination for kids we’ve been here twice now and must say it’s a fantastic place

Great place to stay,
Heaps of accommodation options, 4 pools, kids club, restaurant, mini golf etc.

Had villa by one of the 3 pools that they have, it’s so lovely looking straight out from your windows at the pool & the lovely gardens. The pool & the spa are both heated at this time of year. They are open for use from 7am to 8pm. They also have a splash station for the toddlers mini golf, a jumping balloon, washing machines. You can rent cabins/villas, park up your caper van/ caravan or tent. There are parking spots with your own private bathroom. Or you can use the other toilets and showers. Harbour town is just around the corner. It’s central to everything. We stayed because our daughter was being operated on at Gold Coast university hospital which is 7 minutes away.

Kids club was wonderful and entertaining as usual.

My daughter and I enjoyed a cocktail on the deck while watching the kids.

My grandson is intellectually disabled and the staff are wonderful with him. Nothing is too much trouble and they are always quick to offer a smile.

This is the best park for kids and also parents as they can relax knowing the whole family is catered for.

Oh and they had the Footy on the big screen on the park and there is always plenty of seating available or you can throw a blanket down on the grass!

Looking forward to our next stay in October for all the spooky fun!

We had an issue that was caused by our little one and the issue was fixed straight away by their maintenance person who was very polite and friendly and the staff at reception were great.

They have a bistro there, so we had some Breakfast whilst the kids played nearby which was great and very convenient. If you are parent and want to have a bite to eat or drink whilst the kids play, this is perfect. 10/10 - we will be back.

The only thing I will say and this has nothing to do with the park, is you really need to be extra careful when driving inside the park, the amount of children riding their bikes and scooters with no protective gear on and no parent supervision is insane, on a few occasions kids would come screaming around on their bikes and nearly hitting us whilst driving out and these kids were under 5 or 10, again, Nothing on the park but just something to be mindful off when you visit.

Leaving my original review below because my last visit hasn't entirely changed my mind on this park but it's certainly been a lot less impressive as prior. Big premium parks have high expectations but this one doesn't disappoint. Multiple pools, spas, a splash pad play equipment etc all the stuff for the kids but it hasn't had the 'overflowing' effect many other parks have despite being peak season and from what I can tell, fully booked.

2nd time staying in the hotel for my trip because of the following:- location: 3min drive to Harbour Town Outlet- Amenities: jumping pillow, warm pool, mini golf, go cart, a lot to play with for the kids- Staff: nice and friendly, they taught me how to use the outdoor bbq (never used in my life🤣)- A thing to note that the place is well maintained and very clean, however, I did find that one of the covers of the bunk bed was not replaced and actually smelled like some kid wee on it, which was the only complaint I had for this stay.

Great place so much to do

Disappointed with the Caravan Park’s Attitude Towards Kids

We’ve been coming to NRMA caravan parks for years and always loved the family-friendly vibe — but sadly, things have changed. My kids were excited to do something fun and harmless: sell some homemade lolly bags to other kids around the park. It was just a bit of innocent fun during the holidays, with full supervision and everything done safely.

Unfortunately, they were told to stop immediately — no discussion, just a flat-out “no.” No understanding, no compromise. It’s a real shame to see a place that’s supposed to support families shut down something that builds confidence, social skills, and a bit of old-school holiday fun.

We’re not talking about anything disruptive or unsafe — just a few young kids trying to enjoy the spirit of a caravan park holiday. Disappointed in how this was handled. Not the welcoming, kid-friendly atmosphere we used to love.

One comment was made by staff that you can't operate a business in a business.

The children made $30 i wouldn't call that a business

Apart from that, there are nice daily activities for kids of all ages that are run by the staff, and the pools are very kid friendly. The huge jumping pillow was probably the highlight for our kids.

Very nice Park..very helpful staff . WiFi needs improvement.

activities on more days fantastic resort enjoyed our stay

One of our favourite caravan parks. So much to offer in terms of entertainment. Kids club at the wreck room with amazing staff. Great features of the park like playgrounds, jumping pillow, multiple pools, hot spas, mini golf, and go karts!

Always an amazing time for the whole family especially with the addition of movie nights under the stars, trivia nights and bingo.

Handy restaurant on site. Close to all theme parks.

The whole time my wife and I were there, there was no hot water in the men's amenities block closest to our caravan site, this meant no hot shower. ||After going and informing office staff about this crap on day one and telling them of the inconvenience, the best they could come up with is this. Has changed heaps in last 4 years we have been going same group same time, this year's things removed from last year same price, things feel run down. We going to explore others places but will keep in mind for another time

I have been here alot best place ever and staff are amazing and so nice best place to stay must come here if your in Gold Coast love it hear tips hand rails where stairs are on some places would be good for people who have trouble using stairs and hi.

Erg mooie camping voor het gezin. Echt een aanrader!

We have not long returned from home from our second stay at TI and once again we had the best time!
We arrived the day they re opened after the ex cyclone Alfred, We were informed prior to our travel that the pools Wouldn’t be up and running for quite a few days, Which was okay with us as we were just happy to get away on a holiday.  However to our surprise, the pools were up and running the following day! They must be commended on how quickly they got the resort up and running again. I’m sure it was a stressful time, but everyone had a warm and welcoming smile on their face!

The air-conditioner had also stopped working in one of the bedrooms upstairs, management/maintenance were very quick to provide a cooler until it could be fixed the next day.

The only downside and my only suggestion would be to maybe have an age restriction noted for the ballgames, one game was too grown up for him so we left. However with handball my 5 yo son lined up to play, but was never given a chance to have a go, We waited for awhile and I realised that they were skipping him and I seen his name was crossed off the list, needless to say, I had a very upset little boy. There was a group of kids that were very obviously favoured over other guests, which I’m sure played a part.

We will definitely be back! Especially for Sam’s cocktails and fantastic hospitality 😉

There was 5 of us and for a boys weekend to wet and wild it was very ideal to house us and had enough space for us all.  Bathroom was a great size and felt modern enough.
